content.full_text:
FROM MH&M

12.6.1990 12:00

MARGOLIS, HERTZBERG & MORIN
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
3550 WILSHIRE BOULEVARD
SUITE 1418
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90010-2415
TELEPHONE (213) 381-1121
CABLE: "HERTZLAW"
FACSIMILE: (213) 381-1986

OF COUNSEL
KAREN MOSKOWITZ

VIA FACSIMILE TRANSMISSION
916/327-2026

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: FPPC Agenda - December 4, 1990 - Item 2

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

On Tuesday, December 4, 1990, I appeared before the Commission as a representative for Los Angeles City Councilwoman Gloria Molina. On behalf of Councilwoman Molina, I expressed her strong support for campaign contribution limits and sought a definitive ruling from the Commission concerning the application of Proposition 73 campaign contribution limits in special elections.

Councilwoman Molina is presently a candidate in a special election for Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors. The election is scheduled for January 22, 1991.

In its unanimously adopted motion in support of the Proposition 73 campaign contribution limitations for special elections, the Commission did not indicate whether their action was retroactive prior to December 4th.

Yesterday, I spoke with Mr. Jonathan Rothman of your office, who suggested that I write to you to seek clarification with respect to the retroactive effect of the Commission's action.

The Molina for Supervisor campaign was advised by the FPPC staff, through telephone advice, on more than one occasion, and through more than one staff person, that the recent Court decisions completely lifted the contribution limitations for her Los Angeles County Supervisor's race. Thereafter, to be extraordinarily cautious, the campaign consulted professionals who are expert in the field of campaign law and received the same advice. As a result, the Molina for Supervisor campaign did accept some checks in excess of $1,000.00, from the inception of the campaign until November 21, 1990, when we received telephonic notice, and a copy of Jonathan Rothman's November 21, 1990 Memorandum. Since that date, no funds have been deposited in excess of the $1,000.00 contribution limitation.

FROM MH&M

12.6.1990 12:01

P.3

MARCOLIS, HERTZBERG & MORIN
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Re: FPPC Agenda - December 4, 1990 - Item 2
December 6, 1990
Page 2

There is no doubt that there was a great deal of uncertainty created by the various Court rulings and, as such, the Molina for Supervisor campaign is making this formal inquiry as to whether the Commission's December 4th action is retroactive from the date of such action. Alternatively, inquiry is made as to whether, at a minimum, the retroactive effect of the Commission's action should be from November 21, 1990 forward since that was the date that all of the campaigns received notification of the Commission's intended action.

As you can imagine, we would appreciate your very prompt response.

Very truly yours,

ROBERT M. HERTZBERG

RMH:rmp

cc: Mr. Jonathan Rothman (VIA FACSIMILE TRANSMISSION)
